items.title: NTSB probes controller as part of broader investigation into LaGuardia airport collision
items.summary: NEW YORK/MONTREAL: Investigators probing the deadly collision of an Air Canada Express jet with a fire truck at New York's LaGuardia airport said on Monday they wanted to interview an air traffic controller who was juggling another emergency in the run-up to the crash. "It's pretty traumatic for that air traffic controller as well," she said. "We'll want to interview that air traffic controller as well as others that were in the tower."

items.title: Bill Cosby ordered to pay US$19m over sex abuse claim
items.summary: LOS ANGELES: A woman who said she was drugged and sexually assaulted by veteran US entertainer Bill Cosby was awarded more than US$19 million on Monday after a civil hearing in California. "She knew she had been drugged and raped by Bill Cosby," the suit said. The jury took three days to deliver its verdict, ordering Cosby to pay US$19.3 million, a figure that could increase if they add punitive damages.

items.title: Witnesses recount Penang fireworks stall inferno
items.summary: One worker said the incident unfolded suddenly. Some took cover in the restaurant," the worker, in his 30s, told the New Straits Times. Another worker, who declined to be named, said everything happened suddenly.

items.title: Bookings plummet for Malaysian travel agencies amid Middle East war
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Travel agencies in Malaysia are reporting a sharp drop in bookings. Several agencies said many customers were choosing to postpone or cancel their trips since Feb 28 when the United States and Israel launched a sustained bombing campaign against Iran, which has retaliated with drones and missiles. The United Kingdom, the US, France and Germany are among the biggest sources of tourists for Malaysia outside of Asia.

items.title: Iran denies talks with US after Trump postpones strikes on power grid
items.summary: WASHINGTON/JERUSALEM/TEL AVIV: Iran denied on Monday that it had engaged in negotiations with the US, after President Donald Trump postponed a threat to bomb Iran's power grid because of what he described as productive talks with unidentified Iranian officials. Trump wrote on his Truth Social platform that the US and Iran had held "very good and productive" conversations about a "complete and total resolution of hostilities in the Middle East." As a result, he said, he was postponing for five days a plan to hit Iran's energy grid.

items.title: Foreign tourists extend stays in Malaysia amid Middle East flight disruptions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Foreign tourists are extending their stays in Malaysia as the United States-Israel-Iran conflict disrupts flights and forces travellers from the US, Germany, Turkiye and Australia to reroute their journeys. I do not plan to return to the US for now," he told the New Straits Times. [[nid:1402612]] German tourist Colin Wagner, 29, said returning home now would be costly.

items.title: Israel strikes south Beirut, says captured Hizbollah members
items.summary: HAZMIYEH: Israeli strikes hit Beirut's southern suburbs on Monday, the first attack on the Hizbollah stronghold in days, as its military said it had captured two members of the Iran-backed group in southern Lebanon. An earlier Israeli strike had hit the upscale, predominantly Christian area of Hazmieh near Beirut, with Israel saying it targeted a member of the Iranian Revolutionary Guards' foreign operations arm. The Israeli military told AFP two Hizbollah members were captured.

items.title: Middle East conflict threatens to disrupt Malaysia's travel industry
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tourism stakeholders are closely monitoring the impact of the ongoing war in the Middle East, with one analyst warning of an "extremely horrific period" for Malaysia's airline and travel sectors. Malaysian Association of Tour and Travel Agents president Nigel Wong said the conflict was affecting inbound and outbound tourism, and had resulted in many flight and hotel cancellations. He said many travellers were adopting a wait-and-see approach before confirming their travel plans.

items.title: A slap on the wrist: Existing legal gaps weaken crackdown on syndicated cable theft, among key challenges, says Prasarana
items.summary: “Law enforcement like the Royal Malaysia Police has been very helpful in curbing cable theft but their hands have been tied for a long time. Idzqandar said lenient legal deterrence has resulted in rising cases of cable thefts and repeated offenders. — Picture by Yusof Mat Isa Idzqandar said law enforcement authorities typically investigate cable theft incidents under Section 379 of the Penal Code for theft, but suspects are often ultimately prosecuted under Sections 447 for criminal trespass instead.

items.title: Kim Jong Un says North Korea's nuclear status is irreversible, threatens South
items.summary: SEOUL: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un said his country would permanently strengthen its nuclear forces and treat South Korea as its most hostile state, as he set out policy priorities in a speech to parliament, state media KCNA reported on Tuesday. Kim said Pyongyang's status as a nuclear-armed state was irreversible and expanding a "self-defensive nuclear deterrent" was essential to national security, regional stability and economic development. He rejected the idea that nuclear disarmament could be exchanged for economic benefits or security guarantees, saying North Korea had already proven that maintaining nuclear forces while pursuing development was the correct strategic choice.

items.title: OnlyFans owner Radvinsky dead at 43
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Leonid Radvinsky, the reclusive billionaire who acquired adult content platform OnlyFans and transformed it into a multibillion-dollar empire, has died of cancer aged 43, the company announced Monday. "We are deeply saddened to announce the death of Leo Radvinsky. Leo passed away peacefully after a long battle with cancer," an OnlyFans spokesperson said.

items.title: Trump administration launches more probes into Harvard
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The Trump administration said on Monday it launched two more probes against Harvard University in its latest escalation against the Cambridge, Massachusetts-based school and other top US universities. Harvard task forces released reports last year saying Jewish and Muslim students at the university had faced bigotry and abuse. A deal to resolve the administration's probes against Harvard remains elusive.

items.title: UK police probe attack on Jewish ambulances
items.summary: LONDON: British police said they were investigating an online claim of responsibility for an arson attack after four volunteer ambulances run by a Jewish organisation next to a London synagogue were set on fire early Monday. Counter-terrorism police have been tasked with the inquiry into what Prime Minister Keir Starmer called a "deeply shocking antisemitic arson attack." Police probe Police said in a statement "the arson attack is being treated as an antisemitic hate crime."

items.title: Jury in social media addiction trial tells judge it's having difficulty coming to consensus
items.summary: LOS ANGELES: The jury in the social media addiction trial in Los Angeles told the judge on Monday it is having difficulty coming to a consensus with one defendant. The jury has been deliberating for over a week in a trial that involves a young woman who said she became addicted to Google's YouTube and Meta's Instagram at a young age. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Firework shop blazes up 494pc
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Fires at premises selling firecrackers and fireworks spiked 494 per cent last year, rising from 19 cases in 2024 to 113 nationwide. Fire and Rescue Department director-general Datuk Seri Nor Hisham Mohammad said seven cases were reported up to February this year. "The seven cases include two each in Johor and Penang and one each in Kedah, Kuala Lumpur and Melaka," Nor Hisham said.

items.title: Medicine supply stable, says ministry
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Health Ministry said the supply of medicines remains stable and well managed despite the Middle East conflict. "Stock levels at Health Ministry facilities are maintained for one to three months of usage. "The ministry monitors the medicine supply through existing mechanisms, including a reporting system for supply disruptions managed by the National Pharmaceutical Regulatory Agency."

items.title: Global economy under major threat, energy agency warns
items.summary: SYDNEY: The global economy is under "major threat" from the energy crisis caused by the Middle East war and "no country will be immune" to its effects, International Energy Agency chief Fatih Birol said yesterday. Speaking at the National Press Club in Australia's capital, Birol compared the current energy crisis to those of the 1970s and the impact of Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine. "This crisis as things stand is now two oil crises and one gas crash put all together.

items.title: Kim reappointed as president of state affairs commission
items.summary: SEOUL: North Korea's rubber-stamp legislature has re-elected Kim Jong Un as the president of the State Affairs Commission, state media reported yesterday. The reappointment of Kim as head of the commission — the authoritarian nation's highest policymaking body — was announced by KCNA. "The Supreme People's Assembly of the DPRK re-elected Comrade Kim Jong Un as president of the state affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea at the first session of its 15th term on March 22," KCNA said.

items.title: Volvo SUV engulfed in flames on Federal Highway, black smoke billows
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A Volvo XC60 SUV was destroyed in a fire along the Federal Highway near Mentari Court in Bandar Sunway, officials said. The Fire and Rescue Department said the first engine arrived at 1.57pm, about 6km from the station. The fire was brought under control at 2.10pm.

items.title: Mother and son killed as MPV crashes and plunges down slope in Dungun
items.summary: DUNGUN: A woman and her teenage son were killed when the multi-purpose vehicle (MPV) she was driving crashed at Km 59 of the Jerangau–Jabor Road near Felda Jerangau here today. In the 6.10pm incident, Nurul Hafizah Shaary, 39, and her son, Mohd Redzuan Arash Mohd Redzuan, 13, were pronounced dead at the scene. Bandar Al Muktafi Billah Shah Fire and Rescue Station operations officer Mohd Harith Che Hussin said the department received an emergency call at 6.19pm.

items.title: Finance Ministry warns of benefit ban over Budi95 misuse
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Finance Ministry has warned of a complete withdrawal of benefits for individuals found misusing the Budi95 fuel scheme. "Misusing subsidised fuel is an offence," it said. In the recent case, the ministry said investigations found that the individual involved was a Malaysian and a MyKad holder.

items.title: Russia tightens the screws on the Internet
items.summary: Guy Faulconbridge OFFICE workers toiling with blocked Internet. Teenagers continually forced to switch VPNs, and taxi drivers struggle to find their way around Moscow without online navigation. "These measures are taking place," Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said when asked about restrictions on messaging apps and Internet service.

items.title: War threatens another food price shock in developing world
items.summary: DISRUPTED fertiliser shipments and soaring energy prices from the war in Iran are threatening to unleash a fresh food-price surge across vulnerable nations. Developing countries were strengthening — and attracting investment — after the global pandemic and the Ukraine war sent food, fuel and financial markets into turmoil. "This could have a big impact on food prices over time," said Odile Renaud-Basso, president of the European Bank for Reconstruction and Development (EBRD), a core lender across some 40 emerging economies.

items.title: NST Leader: Eye in the sky to steer Malaysian drivers toward safety
items.summary: In Malaysia, drones are deployed too, but with a civilian mission. These Road Transport Department (RTD)-commissioned drones spotted 2,384 offences in three days of pre-Hari Raya traffic operations beginning March 16. The drones identified four main offences: overtaking on double lines, ignoring traffic lights, overtaking on the left and misusing emergency lanes.

items.title: Four-wheel-drive plunges into Klang River; driver condition unknown
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A four-wheel-drive driver had a frightening moment when his vehicle plunged into the Klang River near Jalan Munsyi Abdullah here this morning. The Kuala Lumpur Civil Defence Force District Operations Control Centre said it received an alert on the incident at 10.40am. A rescue team, together with Emergency Medical Services personnel from the Kuala Lumpur district Civil Defence Force, was immediately deployed to the scene for a rescue operation.

items.title: Fire destroys firecracker stall, car, food truck
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A stall selling firecrackers and fireworks in Jalan Bunga Ros, Kampung Dato' Sulaiman Menteri, here was destroyed in a fire tonight. The blaze also caused a Proton Gen-2 car and a food truck to catch fire after being hit by sparks. "The fire involved a makeshift tent, which was about 90 per cent destroyed.

items.title: Zuckerberg creating AI agent to help him run company
items.summary: MOSCOW: Mark Zuckerberg, CEO of Meta Platforms is developing an artificial intelligence (AI) agent to assist him in managing the company, according to The Wall Street Journal on Monday, citing people familiar with the matter, reported Sputnik/RIA Novosti. Meta Platforms employees have also started using personal AI agents such as My Claw, that can quickly look through their message history and files, the report said. Another AI tool called Second Brain combines chatbot and agent functions, The Wall Street Journal reported.

items.title: Oil palm plantation fire destroys 60ha in Pahang state
items.summary: KUANTAN: Some 60ha of an oil palm plantation in Kampung Jemeri, Kuala Rompin, were destroyed in a fire this afternoon. A Pahang Fire and Rescue Department spokesman said the department received an emergency call at 2.41pm, and a team from the Kuala Rompin Fire and Rescue Station arrived at the scene at 3.15pm. The operation involved nine firefighters and four plantation workers, using a lorry and two four-wheel-drive vehicles.

items.title: Major highways see heavy congestion as festive return peaks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Traffic congestion was reported on major highways nationwide at 8.30pm tonight following a surge in vehicles as city dwellers returned from their hometowns. A Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) spokesperson said the congestion was due to heavy traffic flow from the north, south and east coast heading towards the federal capital. "However, traffic at the Sungai Besi Toll Plaza remained smooth so far," he said.

items.title: Court orders wood processing company to pay RM476k to TNB over meter tampering
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Klang Sessions Court has ordered a wood processing company to pay RM476,124.65 in compensation to Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) over meter tampering. Judge Mohd Sabri Ismail made the ruling after allowing the suit filed by the electricity utility company against Top Status KD Sdn Bhd. In his grounds of judgment dated March 18, Mohd Sabri dismissed the defendant's argument that the sharp drop in electricity consumption was due to the closure of factory operations during the Movement Control Order (MCO) following the Covid-19 pandemic.

items.title: Today's news in pictures - Monday, March 23, 2026
items.summary: MISSED what happened today? [[nid:1402509]] SEPANG – The sun rises over Sepang as MetMalaysia reports heatwave conditions (Level 2) in several states with temperatures reaching 37°C to 40°C. NSTP/MOHD FADLI HAMZAH [[nid:1402508]] NILAI – Traffic increases toward Kuala Lumpur on the North-South Expressway as city dwellers return from their Hari Raya Aidilfitri holiday break.

items.title: MOH confirms civil servants caught smoking at Rawang eatery, compounds imposed
items.summary: The MOH said compounds have been imposed on the individuals under Section 16(2) (No 4: smoking at eating places) of the Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Act 2024 (Act 852). Earlier today, a post went viral on X depicting individuals serving with the MOH smoking at a restaurant in Rawang. “The MOH greatly appreciates the public’s concerns and encourages the channelling of information or complaints through official MOH channels to strengthen enforcement to protect public health,” it added.

items.title: Sultan of Pahang urges public to stay vigilant amid hot, dry weather
items.summary: KUANTAN, March 23 — The Sultan of Pahang Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri’ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah has urged the public to take extra precautions amid the current hot and dry weather affecting the state. Al-Sultan Abdullah also urged the public to refrain from open burning, including the disposal of rubbish, dry leaves and garden waste, as it could trigger uncontrolled fires and worsen air quality. “Open burning is not only dangerous but can also cause haze and affect public health.

items.title: KL-Butterworth ETS delayed by over 2.5 hours after technical glitch at Rasa station
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 23 — Electric Train Service (ETS) EP9130 from KL Sentral to Butterworth is expected to experience a delay of about 157 minutes following a technical issue at Rasa station, Hulu Selangor. Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB) said in a statement that due to the technical glitch, the train, which departed KL Sentral at 1.40 pm, is now expected to reach Butterworth Station at 8.22 pm instead of the original 5.45 pm. “The technical glitch at Rasa station occurred at 3.02 pm today,” said KTMB.

items.title: Former minister Yeo Bee Yin and husband announce arrival of identical twin sons
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 23 — Puchong Member of Parliament Yeo Bee Yin and her husband, Lee Yeow Seng, have announced the arrival of their identical twin boys. In her social media post, Yeo shared details about the twins, who are classified as monochorionic diamniotic (MCDA), meaning they share a single placenta but develop in separate amniotic sacs. “Yup, two more boys have joined the LeeBro team.

items.title: Elderly man dies, five injured in two-vehicle crash near Gerik-Jeli highway R&R
items.summary: IPOH, March 23 — An elderly man was killed while five others were injured in a collision involving two vehicles near the Puncak Titiwangsa Rest and Service (R&R) area on the Gerik-Jeli East-West Highway (JRTB) today. Perak Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) assistant director of operations Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said the department received an emergency call regarding the incident at 11.24 am. He said a team of 17 personnel and five fire tenders from the Gerik and Jeli Fire and Rescue Stations were dispatched to the scene.

items.title: Heavy traffic reported on major highways as Malaysians return to Kuala Lumpur after Aidilfitri
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 23 — Traffic on several major highways nationwide was heavy as of 5 pm today as city dwellers began returning to the capital after the Aidilfitri holidays. A Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) spokesperson said congestion along the North-South Expressway (PLUS) E1 affected both northbound and southbound directions due to a surge in return traffic ahead of the resumption of work tomorrow. “Traffic on the Kuala Lumpur-Karak Expressway (KLK) was congested only in the westbound direction (towards Kuala Lumpur), while eastbound traffic remained smooth,” the spokesperson said.

items.title: Rising voters in Tawau spark plan to to create second parliamentary seat
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 23 — Tawau could be divided into two parliamentary constituencies following a sharp increase in the number of registered voters in the area, according to Datuk Nizam Abu Bakar Titingan. Sabah Youth, Sports and Creative Economy Development Minister said the current Tawau parliamentary seat has more than 80,000 voters, highlighting the need for better representation, The Star reported today. The Tawau federal seat also covers three state constituencies: Apas, Balung and Sri Tanjong.

items.title: As temperatures soar, Kedah and Perlis worry about drying rivers and shrinking reservoirs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 23 — Residents in northern peninsular Malaysia are facing severe water shortages and rising temperatures as a prolonged heatwave continues to grip Kedah and Perlis. In Kampung Lubuk Tualang, Sik, Sungai Tebal is running unusually shallow, exposing sandbanks in several areas, while local hill water sources have dried up, forcing villagers to ration their limited supplies, Harian Metro reported today. The extreme heat is also affecting agriculture, with rice fields outside the Muda Agricultural Development Authority (Mada) areas reporting poor yields due to limited irrigation.

items.title: Kedah police checking hospitals after viral claims of ‘mercun bola’ injuries in Pendang
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, March 23 — Police are obtaining further information from the hospital regarding claims that several individuals were injured after being hit by a ‘mercun bola’ (ball firecracker) in Tobiar, Pendang. Police will visit the hospital today to obtain full details regarding the incident. Earlier, the incident went viral on Facebook, showing pictures of several injured individuals with the caption: ‘tragedi pakar bom dari Tobiar, Pendang, Kedah..ujikaji custom mercun bola, tujuh mangsa beraya di hospital’ (‘Tragedy involving bomb expert from Tobiar, Pendang, Kedah… custom ball firecracker experiment, seven victims celebrating Aidilfitri in hospital).

items.title: Bukit Mertajam Hospital dismisses claims of relocation to avoid disrupting temple
items.summary: SEBERANG PERAI, March 23 — Bukit Mertajam Hospital’s Board of Visitors today dismissed allegations that the hospital would be relocated to avoid disrupting a temple. Board spokesperson Ng Yi Cheah, who lodged a police report today over the false claims, said the allegations appeared on social media on March 22. “The post titled ‘Hospital Bukit Mertajam akan dipindahkan agar tak ganggu kuil - Steven Sim Chee Keong’ was false, baseless, misleading and malicious in intent,” he said in a statement after filing the report at the Seberang Perai Tengah police headquarters.

items.title: Bangladeshi man drowns after falling into river in Perak
items.summary: IPOH, March 23 — A Bangladeshi man was found drowned after falling into the river near Kledang Timur 4, Taman Bandar Baru Menglembu, here, earlier this morning. Perak Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said the 29-year-old’s body was recovered after the department received an emergency call from the public at 8.35 am. He said 10 personnel and two fire engines from the Pasir Puteh Fire and Rescue Station were deployed to the scene.

items.title: Johor Immigration cites extensive borders, evolving syndicate tactics as key challenges
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 23 — Johor’s geographical challenges, with its extensive land and maritime borders, as well as the increasingly deceptive tactics used by illegal immigrant syndicates, which frequently change their modus operandi, are among the main challenges faced by the state Immigration Department in curbing illegal activities. State Immigration director Datuk Mohd Rusdi Mohd Darus said these factors have led to the existence of numerous ‘rat trails’ (illegal routes) along the coastline, with areas such as Kota Tinggi, Mersing and Pontian becoming primary targets for syndicates smuggling illegal immigrants from Indonesia. Declining to reveal the exact hotspot locations, he said that the syndicates take advantage of illegal sea routes, using small boats at night to bring in illegal immigrants, especially from Indonesia.

items.title: Health Ministry: Malaysia’s medicine supply stable despite West Asia conflict
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 23 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) has confirmed that the supply of medicines in the country is currently stable and under control despite the conflict in West Asia. In a statement today, MOH said that the short-term impact of the West Asia conflict is minimal based on current assessments. “The stock level of medicines at MOH facilities is maintained at between one and three months based on usage requirements, and is supported by additional buffer stock of up to two months at the concession company level for medicines supplied through the logistics concession,” it said.

items.title: After 40 years in the dark, Marudi village lights up just in time for Raya
items.summary: Deputy Minister in the Sarawak Premier’s Department (Labour, Immigration and Project Monitoring) Datuk Gerawat Gala said the electricity connection to the village was completed two weeks ago, just in time for the Raya celebration. “Kampung Seberang Lubok Nibong holds a special place in my heart as it is the only Muslim village in DUN N.78 Mulu,” he said, adding that the village has about 60 residents. Also present was the village headman Madlie Moss.

items.title: Future of Kapit seat: Nanta signals Alvin Runggah as next parliamentary candidate
items.summary: KAPIT, March 23 — Kapit MP Dato Sri Alexander Nanta Linggi has signalled that Datuk Alvin Runggah Jimbun is the person he intends to entrust with the Kapit parliamentary seat if he no longer contests in future elections. “If the time comes when I no longer contest as a parliamentary candidate for P215 Kapit, Datuk Alvin Runggah Jimbun will be the individual who will take over that responsibility. “Therefore, he needs the full support of the Kapit community to ensure that development continuity can be carried forward successfully,” he said during a Thanksgiving Dinner here on Sunday.

items.title: From 82 to 99? Sarawak assembly approves 17 new state seats, but Election Commission has final say
items.summary: KUCHING, March 23 — The Sarawak Legislative Assembly (DUN) has approved an increase in the number of state seats, expanding from 82 to 99 following the addition of 17 new constituencies. “At this stage, there is no report yet from SPR on whether the 17 new constituencies have been confirmed. However, increasing parliamentary seats would require a two-thirds majority in Parliament, which may be more challenging for the ruling government.

items.title: Soldier killed in crash involving two motorcycles and car in Alor Gajah
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH, March 23 — A soldier was killed in a crash involving two motorcycles and a car along Jalan Padang Kemunting, Masjid Tanah here, early this morning. Melaka Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department (JSPT) chief Supt Muhammad Zaki Ramat said the crash also involved a SYM VF3i motorcycle and a Proton Saga car. “He then collided with a motorcycle and a car travelling in the opposite direction, from Pengkalan Balak towards Sungai Udang.

items.title: NRES strengthens weather monitoring with AI, big data to tackle climate change
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 23 — The Ministry of Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability (NRES), through the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia), is strengthening weather monitoring and forecasting capabilities to ensure the country remains prepared for climate change. Its minister, Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup, said the effort includes integrating artificial intelligence (AI) and big data analytics into the Multi-Hazard Early Warning System (MHEWS), in line with current climate challenges. Arthur said strengthening the National Climate Centre of Excellence is also a key focus to support research on climate change impacts and guide the development of more sustainable and resilient national adaptation policies.

items.title: Penang to launch first direct flight from Qingdao, boosting tourism and trade links
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 23 — Penang is set to achieve a significant milestone with the launch of its first direct flight connecting Qingdao, China and the state on March 31, marking a major step in enhancing air connectivity between Malaysia and China. State Tourism and Creative Economy Committee chairman Wong Hon Wai said that the new route, to be operated by Qingdao Airlines, represents a crucial development in strengthening bilateral ties, particularly in tourism, trade and cultural exchanges “With the introduction of this route, Qingdao will become the northernmost city in China currently connected to Penang via direct flights. “This important breakthrough reflects the steady northward expansion of Penang’s air connectivity with China, progressively enhancing our network coverage and strengthening overall connectivity,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Safety first, celebration second: Housing minister orders review after seventh fireworks stall fire destroys property
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 23 — Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming has ordered a review of standard operating procedures (SOPs) for temporary fireworks stalls following a fire in Nibong Tebal, Penang yesterday. Both the Fire and Rescue Department and the Local Government Department were directed to conduct the review, The New Straits Times reported today. According to The New Straits Times , the Taman Pekaka fire is the seventh fire involving fireworks stalls reported in Malaysia this year.

items.title: Anwar wishes Sultan Ibrahim health and blessings on official birthday
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 23 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today conveyed his congratulations in conjunction with the official birthday of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia. “I humbly extend my highest congratulations and best wishes in conjunction with the official birthday of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, Sultan and Ruler of Johor Darul Ta’zim and the King of Malaysia, on March 23, 2026, corresponding to 3 Syawal 1447 Hijrah. “May Allah protect the Sultan,” he said.

items.title: Zahid thanks meteorologists for safeguarding public safety on World Meteorological Day
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 23 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi expressed his gratitude to all meteorologists nationwide for their commitment to ensuring public safety through continuous monitoring and the delivery of accurate weather information, in conjunction with World Meteorological Day 2026, observed today. “Today, I would like to express my deep appreciation to all meteorologists, especially the personnel of the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia), for their commitment in continuously monitoring, analysing and delivering weather information to us,” he said in a Facebook post today. Themed ‘Observing Today, Protecting Tomorrow’, he said that the role of meteorology is becoming increasingly important in ensuring that every step taken today is able to protect the lives and well-being of the people in the future.

items.title: Sabah’s 40pc revenue claim negotiations cannot be rushed, says former Rela state honorary president
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 23 — Former honorary president of Persatuan Ahli-Ahli Kehormat Ikatan Relawan Rakyat Malaysia (Rela) Negeri Sabah, Datuk Jerry Ambuting Mohd Said, said Tuaran MP Datuk Wilfred Madius Tangau’s statement regarding the “countdown” for Sabah’s 40 per cent revenue claim does not reflect the full scope of the ongoing negotiation process. According to Jerry, negotiations based on the Malaysia Agreement 1963 are conducted through official channels, including the MA63 Consultative Council Technical Committee, which requires in-depth review and careful legal and fiscal consideration. “The negotiation process also takes into account various aspects, including financial implications to the country and the shared interests between the Federation and Sabah.

items.title: Police probe Nibong Tebal firecracker stall blaze, MBSP revokes permit
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 23 — The police have launched an investigation into a fire involving a firecracker and fireworks stall in Taman Pekaka, Nibong Tebal, yesterday. Penang police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said five reports have been lodged so far. Investigations are ongoing,” he said when contacted today.

items.title: Durian trees bloom early in Penang as heatwave sparks hopes of longer, cheaper season
items.summary: BALIK PULAU, March 23 — Weeks of dry, hot weather have pushed durian trees in Penang into early bloom this year, and orchard owners say if conditions stay favourable, the harvest could stretch longer with more competitive prices by mid‑May into June. The rest will come from end‑April to mid‑August,” orchard owner Tan Chee Keat, 35, was quoted as saying. Tan added that the prolonged dry spell has helped flowering, but also required careful orchard management.

items.title: Malaysia to participate in WHO pandemic preparedness talks virtually amid West Asia conflict
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 23 — Malaysia will participate in the 6th Intergovernmental Working Group (IGWG6) negotiation session at the World Health Organisation in Geneva, Switzerland, from today until Saturday, via an online platform due to the conflict in West Asia. The Ministry of Health (MOH), in a statement, said the participation involves collaboration with several relevant government agencies, including the Attorney General’s Chambers. MOH said Malaysia recognises the importance of the Pathogen Access and Benefit-Sharing System (PABS) negotiations in strengthening global pandemic preparedness.

items.title: Three-storey furniture shop in Bukit Bintang destroyed in fire, no casualties reported
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 23 — A three-storey furniture shop in Bukit Bintang, here was destroyed in a fire last night. The Kuala Lumpur Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) operations centre said it received an emergency call on the incident at 10.58pm. “The fire affected the ground and second floors of the building, which housed furniture and perfume businesses, with damage estimated at 70 per cent,” JBPM said in a statement.

items.title: Hot and dry weather hits Padang Terap in Kedah at heatwave level as public urged to stay indoors
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, March 23 — Kedahans have been urged to limit outdoor activities and reduce exposure to direct sunlight following the hot and dry weather currently affecting most parts of the country. The water levels in rivers and dams in this state are also decreasing, so avoid wasting water. “There are areas facing water shortages, so do not waste water.

items.title: Smooth like butter: Klang Valley-bound traffic steady, highway authority warns of afternoon jams
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 23 — Traffic on major highways heading towards the Klang Valley is smooth and under control this morning. A spokesman for the Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) said the agency expects traffic to increase from noon today as people begin returning after celebrating Hari Raya Aidilfitri in their hometowns. According to him, PLUS has also activated smart lanes at 30 locations from 7 am to 11 pm today to help ease traffic flow.
